name: Chaos Toolkit
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Chaos Engineering extensions via plugins

auth.notes: CLI tool; auth via extension drivers (Kubernetes RBAC, AWS IAM, etc.)

performance.notes: Experiment execution speed depends on target system and probes

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source chaos engineering framework. JSON/YAML experiment definitions with probes, actions, and rollbacks. Extensible via Python plugins. Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, Azure extensions. Structured experiment approach: hypothesis → method → rollback. CLI execution with JSON experiment logs. More principled than ad-hoc chaos scripts.

gotchas: Requires writing JSON/YAML experiment definitions — learning curve
Extensions maintained separately from core toolkit (version compatibility issues)
No built-in safety mechanisms — experiments must be carefully designed
Less polished than commercial tools (Gremlin, Steadybit)
best_when: Teams wanting open-source principled chaos engineering framework
CI/CD pipelines testing system resilience before deployment
Kubernetes-native chaos testing via chaostoolkit-kubernetes extension
Organizations building chaos engineering practices without commercial tooling budget
avoid_when: Teams wanting polished UI and safety guardrails (use Gremlin or Steadybit)
Production systems requiring careful blast radius control (less mature safety features)
Teams without DevOps/SRE expertise to design safe experiments
summary: Chaos Toolkit is an open-source chaos engineering framework with structured JSON/YAML experiment definitions (hypothesis → method → rollback). Extensible Python plugins for Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, Azure. CLI execution. More principled approach than ad-hoc chaos scripts. Best open-source option for teams building chaos engineering practices. Less polished than commercial tools but free and extensible.
